title: Scotland 0-3 Brazil Stats: Vinícius Júnior Brace Seals Top Spot in Group C | Opta Analyst
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(apify:website-content-crawler) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Scotland 0-3 Brazil Stats: Vinícius Júnior Brace Seals Top Spot in Group C
Brazil profited from a slow Scotland start and then went from strength to strength to secure top spot in Group C with a convincing 3-0 win at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Vinícius Júnior scored twice to guide Brazil to top spot in Group C and into the round of 32 at the World Cup with a 3-0 win over Scotland.
Brazil were not quite assured of a top-two finish, but got the win they needed to stay ahead of Morocco in the standings thanks to a strong first half in Miami.
Vinícius Jr got his brace before half-time – having a third goal ruled out – with Matheus Cunha securing the victory on the hour, and Scotland now face a nervous wait to see if they are one of the best third-placed teams to progress to the last 32.
Scotland played themselves into trouble seven minutes in as a dawdling Scott McKenna was closed down by Rayan, who stepped in to tee up Vinícius as he rounded Angus Gunn and slotted into an empty net.
Another Scotland mistake looked to have gifted Vinícius a second after Jack Hendry had his pocket picked on the edge of the box. But the VAR deemed the 25-year-old had fouled the defender – having stepped across him – and Scotland were given a reprieve.
Brazil upped the pressure at the end of the first half, with Lewis Ferguson clearing a poked Cunha effort off the line before an unmarked Vinícius peeled away at the back post to nod in Bruno Guimarães’ cross in the third minute of first-half stoppage time.
Gunn then made himself big to block Rayan’s low curler on the stroke of half-time, but it was Scotland who had the first chance after the break as Scott McTominay headed straight at Alisson.
Vinícius saw his chance for a hat-trick smothered by Gunn, but Cunha coolly slotted past the goalkeeper on the hour mark after Guimarães unselfishly picked him out.
Scotland did not sit back, though, with Alisson forced to push behind Ferguson’s free-kick before scrambling to keep out another McTominay header from the resulting corner.
Neymar’s long-awaited return came in the 76th minute, as he entered as a substitute in a World Cup match for the first time, and he slid in Vinícius for what could have been a hat-trick goal, if not for another sharp Gunn save.
Scotland almost pulled back what could have been a vital consolation in the 95th minute, but McTominay’s poked effort from a recycled corner was held by Alisson.
Expectation Increases for Brazil as Vinícius Shines
Scotland started well in possession, but they crumbled under Brazil’s press, with Vinícius the main beneficiary, and he may feel aggrieved to not walk away with the match ball after having one ruled out for a soft foul.
Three of his five goals at the World Cup have been openers for the Seleção, and he has now been involved in more than half of Brazil’s goals across the last two World Cups, contributing to eight of their 15 goals (five goals, three assists).
Vinícius is also the fifth Brazilian player to score in all three group-stage matches of a single World Cup finals, following in the footsteps of Jairzinho (1970), Romario (1994) and both Ronaldo Nazario and Rivaldo (2002); Brazil went on to win the trophy on each of those three previous occasions.
Brazil were truly on top in the first half as they attempted 11 shots, their highest tally before half-time in a World Cup match since 2018 (11 vs Mexico in the last 16).
Guimarães was also effective, providing two assists in a single match for the first time for them, creating three chances overall (only Lucas Paquetá and Rayan had more – both four).
For Scotland, the eight last-32 qualifiers from the third-place table could come down to goal difference, and this result has done a bit of damage to their chances.
Our Opta match centre delivers you all the Scotland vs Brazil stats from their 2026 FIFA World Cup Group C meeting at Miami Stadium on 24 June.
The match centre below includes team and player stats, expected goals data, passing networks, an Opta chalkboard and more. It gives you everything you need to do your own match analysis.
Underneath the match centre you can find the official Opta stats on the game as well.
Scotland 0-3 Brazil: Post-Match Opta Facts
Brazil have topped their first-round group in each of the FIFA World Cup tournaments since 1982, last finishing second in 1978 behind Austria.
Scotland are now the only opponent that Brazil have faced 11+ occasions in all competitions without ever losing (W9 D2).
Vinícius Júnior’s four goals in 2026 are tied for the most by a Brazilian player at a single first group stage in the FIFA World Cup, alongside Ronaldo (2002), Neymar (2014) and Jairzinho (1970). He has been involved in eight of the last 15 goals for Brazil across the last two tournaments.
Bruno Guimarães has had a goal involvement in six of his last 11 matches for Brazil in all competitions. He is also the player with the most assists for Brazil since 2022 (11) and the second player to have multiple assists for Brazil in a FIFA World Cup match in the 21st century, after Kaká against Ivory Coast in 2010.
With Scott McTominay’s header in the 49th minute, Scotland ended a drought of 200 minutes without a shot on target in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, since John McGinn’s goal against Haiti on Matchday 1.
Carlo Ancelotti is the first Italian manager to reach the knockout stages of the FIFA World Cup since Fabio Capello did so in 2010 for England.
Neymar has become just the fourth player to represent Brazil at four different FIFA World Cup finals, joining Djalma Santos, Cafu, and Pelé. He came on as a substitute for the first time in the tournament (14th appearance), 981 days after he had last played for Brazil (October 2023).
